title: Diplopterygium maximum
taxonomy: Diplopterygium maximum is classified within the family of plants known as Gleicheniaceae, which is a group of ferns. It belongs to the order Gleicheniales and the class Polypodiopsida. This fern species is native to tropical regions and is often found in humid environments. The classification of Diplopterygium maximum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Gleicheniaceae family. By studying its classification, botanists can gain insights into the plant's biology and ecology.

Its medicinal uses include being employed to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
